repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec/v1 is scalar Chebyshev partial-ID under factor misspecification, not a distributional quantile-factor point-ID boundary

published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo: weighted Wasserstein/quantile synthetic control, not finite-donor rank-factor row-span identification

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec/v1 is scalar misspecification-tube partial ID, not open-set distributional rank-factor escape

published_prior_art.cite: Chen2020DistributionalSC and FermanPinto2021ImperfectFit address poor/imperfect fit, not exact-pre-fit rank-factor nonidentification envelopes
Gunsilius2023DiSCo + AbadieDiamondHainmueller2010: stable convex-weight corner is known; this states it as a sufficient rank-factor subcase

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Update the Masini bibliography metadata to Journal of Econometrics volume 249, Part A, article 105675, while keeping the bibkey stable.
Add one sentence in Conjecture 1 explaining exactly how the augmented row-span certificate is computed rank-by-rank from observable donor/pre-period quantile panels.
Strengthen Conjecture 2 by naming a minimal nondegenerate witness class, e.g. fixed small J,T0,R with monotone affine perturbations, before the general relative-open-set statement.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0 with Conjectures 1-2 as the flagship kernel and Theorem 2 treated only as a sanity-preserving sufficient corner.
